title: Setup
body:
Like any Blender addon, this one is just a ZIP file that you import in Preferences. Once enabled, it adds a Character Maker section in the Tools panel. That is the interface used to launch the setup.

When the Load Scene boolean is enabled, the interface icons and all the 3D elements of the portrait that make up the character editor are imported.

title: NOTE — Interface
body: To make the interface easier to understand, each editable category of elements has its own matching greyed-out icon. The different parts of this icon are highlighted when the corresponding parameter is edited.

title: Rendering and Shape Keys
listing: Rigging and animation of the portrait
body:
Once the character customization is satisfactory, the user can rig and animate it.

To demonstrate this, I went through the full process, including an essential retopology and UV unwrapping step, so the bust could be used for rigging, texturing, and animation.

title: NOTE — Shape Keys
body: The face and eyelashes use the same Shape Keys. These are synchronized so that changes remain consistent across all facial elements.

title: Ear Customization
body:
The ears are simple models in several variants, applied through a boolean and then smoothed into the face, which creates a soft transition between the two elements.

The earrings are moved according to the ear type, adapting to each one's length and shape.

title: NOTE — Shape Keys
body: Some of the Shape Keys deforming the ears are the same ones that deform the face and eyelashes, which means that changing one ear parameter applies to all elements that need deformation, in a synchronized way.

title: Eye Customization
body:
The eyes are the most customizable elements of the portrait. In addition to being able to edit their tilt and spacing, you can switch between several pupil and eyelash variations, each following the eyes' global deformations.

The pupils are alpha-based spheres skin-wrapped around the eyeballs. They are constrained by an animatable controller that rotates them, mimicking pupil movement inside the eye without deforming them.

title: Eyebrow Customization
body:
The eyebrows are fully editable through Shape Keys that deform them. This makes it possible to fine-tune their shape, direction, and tilt.

Their original form, offered in three variants, is built as a plane that is then shaped with several modifiers to give it thickness while keeping the Shape Key changes easy to manage.

title: NOTE — Shape Keys
body: Some of the Shape Keys that deform the eyebrows are the same ones used for the face and eyelashes, so editing one eyebrow parameter affects every element that needs deformation, in a synchronized way.

title: Hair Customization
body:
Hair customization is available in two formats. The first lets you switch between several prebuilt complex hairstyle variants. These are detailed but not editable.

To go further into hairstyle editing, you just need to enable the Hair Detailed Options boolean, which then allows the base, fringe, strands, and back of the hair to be modified.

This second option, far more customizable, is made possible by a modeling workflow built around multiple booleans that fuse the hairstyle parts into a single one.
